widgetMetadata.instructions: Each entry under `widgets` describes one chart/widget rendered on this page of the Israeli Ecosystem dashboard. Descriptions are result-agnostic: they explain what the visualization shows and how to interpret it, not the specific numbers. The `dataPaths` array on each entry lists dot-notation paths inside this same JSON file where the underlying data for that widget lives (e.g. "kpis.patentingStartups" or "ipIntensity.items") When summarizing or answering questions about a number from this snapshot, locate the relevant widget(s) by `dataPaths` and quote the corresponding `description` to explain the metric. Widgets that have a 10 year trend usually show the total overall (across all years) and you get the exact data for each year in the past 10 years. The 10 year data do not add up to the total of course

jobs:map.title: Hiring Map
jobs:map.description: Geographic distribution of open job postings across Israel. Each city marker is sized by the number of open roles located there and colored by its administrative district. The legend lists the same totals by district. Selecting a city or district filters the Open Roles list and Hiring Companies to that location. Postings with no resolvable city (remote or unknown) are excluded from the markers and reported separately.

jobs:job-list.title: Open Roles
jobs:job-list.description: Scrollable list of open job postings at Israeli companies. Each row is one currently-available role with its company, title, seniority, function, employment type, and city or remote flag. Only postings flagged as accepting applications and tagged to Israel are included.

jobs:company-list.title: Hiring Companies
jobs:company-list.description: Israeli companies with at least one open role in this snapshot, each with its domain, industry and sub-industry classification, and open-role count. Selecting a company filters the Open Roles list to that company.

jobs:industries.title: Company Industries
jobs:industries.description: Two linked filters. The top strip lists the six top-level industries, each chip labelled with its open-role count; selecting one narrows the sub-industry cloud below it. The cloud shows every sub-industry with open roles — chip size and font scale with posting volume, and the dot color reflects the parent industry. Both levels multi-select and filter all other widgets.

jobs:seniority-matrix.title: Departments × Seniority
jobs:seniority-matrix.description: Heatmap grid crossing functional department (rows) with seniority tier (columns: Unmapped, Entry, Mid, Senior, Director, Executive — Mid collects the LinkedIn Associate tier plus Mid-Senior postings without a senior-cue title; Senior is Mid-Senior postings whose title carries Senior/Sr/Staff/Principal/Lead/Manager). LinkedIn job-function tags are normalized into clean department categories; seniority levels are collapsed into six ordinal tiers. Cell shading is normalized per column so sparse senior tiers stay legible against the dominant Mid-Senior column. Click a cell, a department row, or a seniority column to multi-select and filter the Open Roles list.
